summary.intent: Fix new PR review comments on PR #636 in the moltis repo, specifically by extracting the OAuth redirect URI normalization into a testable pure helper function and adding unit tests for it.
summary.outcome: Extracted normalization logic into a standalone helper function, added 7 unit tests covering the helper, all 93 tests pass (86 existing + 7 new), formatted and clippy-clean, committed and pushed.

repo: OAuth provider setup lives in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs; OAuth config loading is in crates/oauth/src/config_dir.rs and crates/config/src/loader.rs
The project uses cargo workspaces; per-crate tests run with `cargo test -p moltis-provider-setup`
Parallel test execution makes env var mutation (e.g. MOLTIS_OAUTH_{PROVIDER}_REDIRECT_URI) unsafe in tests — pure function extraction is the preferred pattern
The built-in default redirect URI is http://localhost:1455/auth/callback (openai-codex provider)
The has_registered_redirect guard expects pre-configured URIs to already be loopback-normalized by the time they reach it

code.finding: normalize_redirect_uri helper extracted here; called immediately after load_oauth_config in LiveProviderSetupService::oauth_start; unit tests added in the existing test module at the bottom of the file

workflow: Greptile review comments were replied to directly via GitHub API after each fix to mark them resolved
CI status was checked after each push to confirm no regressions
Format + clippy checks were run as a final gate before committing

friction: Env var mutation approach for testing was considered but rejected due to test parallelism races, requiring a refactor to pure function extraction instead
open_items: CI was still re-running against the new head commit at session end — final green status not confirmed in transcript
No other outstanding review feedback noted, but PR #636 was not yet merged
